valorant.last_update_card:
## Valorant â€“ Patch 12.06

### TL;DR
Waylay's Saturate was changed from an INSTANT cast to an EQUIP cast, stripping the ability's most frustrating no-counterplay combos and signaling that Riot considers her dominant enough to deserve the first direct nerf of Act 2. The rest of the patch is backend polish â€” Viper's Pit geometry consistency and a Flex movement speed fix â€” with no weapon balance touches.

### What Got Buffed
- **Viper (Viper's Pit â€” consistency fix)** â€” The chemical cloud's spread around map geometry was reworked to eliminate cases where the ult would clip or fail near elevated surfaces, doorways, and corners (Haven A Tower, Icebox A Pipes specifically called out). Reliable Pit lineups that were quietly broken now work correctly. Verdict: **Worth trying** â€” if your Viper Pit lineups on Icebox A or Haven A have been inconsistent, retest them.

- **Flex items (movement speed)** â€” Equipping a Flex item now matches full Melee movement speed instead of the slower Ability speed (~+11% speed increase while Flex equipped). Removes the real in-game incentive to unequip mid-rotation. Verdict: **Situational** â€” removes a friction point that was training bad habits at higher ranks.

### What Got Nerfed
- **Waylay (Saturate â€” E ability)** â€” Cast type changed from INSTANT to EQUIP. Before: she could deploy Saturate mid-air, reactively, or as an instant combo with zero setup window for opponents. After: requires a wind-up commit, exposing herself during cast. Solo-carry potential drops noticeably â€” she can no longer apply Hinder from angles where enemies have no time to respond. Verdict: **Still playable** â€” coordinated Waylay with proper team setup remains strong. Solo-queue ceiling is meaningfully lower.

### What's OP Right Now
1. **Miks (Initiator)** â€” Introduced in 12.05, received only bug fixes in 12.06. No nerf signal yet = green light for ranked. Play her while Riot is still collecting data.
2. **Tejo** â€” Also bug-fixes only, no adjustments. Still the dominant initiator from Act 2 and nothing changed that.
3. **Viper** â€” The Pit consistency fix quietly restores elite-level site denial on Icebox and Haven. Double-controller comps running Viper are still the play.
4. **Waylay (structured play only)** â€” Still top-tier when Saturate is used as a setup tool with team coordination, not a reactive solo tool.

### What's Dead
Nothing was gutted this patch. 12.06 is a precision adjustment, not a sweep. Waylay's *solo-carry abuse* is dead, but the agent isn't.

### Under the Radar
The Flex movement speed change sounds cosmetic â€” but at high rank, the trained habit of switching to knife before rotating was a real reflex built around the old speed penalty. Removing that friction changes micro-decision-making in post-plant and rotation scenarios. It also signals Riot is willing to revisit cosmetic items that create unintended gameplay side effects, which could matter more as Flex items become more prevalent.

### Meta Verdict
Patch 12.06 doesn't reshape the meta â€” it refines it. Act 2's direction holds: mobile initiators (Miks, Tejo) paired with reliable controllers (Viper, especially post-fix) is the winning formula this week. With Waylay's instant-cast abuse trimmed, teams that built around her uncoordinated pressure need to adjust. Viper is the sleeper pick to abuse right now â€” her Pit is more reliable on Icebox and Haven than it's been in weeks. If you want to climb this week, play Miks or Tejo, run Viper alongside, and only play Waylay if you have a coordinated team that can use Saturate as a setup tool.

---
valorant.tldr: Waylay's Saturate was changed from an INSTANT cast to an EQUIP cast, stripping the ability's most frustrating no-counterplay combos and signaling that Riot considers her dominant enough to deserve the first direct nerf of Act 2. The rest of the patch is backend polish â€” Viper's Pit geometry consistency and a Flex movement speed fix â€” with no weapon balance touches.

fortnite.last_update_card:
## Fortnite âš¡ Patch 40.10

### TL;DR
The April 1 drop is half chaos, half nostalgia â€” OG Chapter 1 Season 8 goes live today with the Volcano, Lazy Lagoon, Pirate Cannons, and the Flint-Knock back in rotation. The Chaos Reloader gets tapped down and the Twin Mag SMG gets a significant movement accuracy buff, quietly reshuffling the weapon meta heading into mid-season.

### What Got Buffed
**Twin Mag SMG â€” Movement Spread Reduced**
Spread while firing, jumping, sprinting, and sliding has been tightened. This is a direct buff to aggressive, mobile SMG play â€” the gun was already strong in close range, now it punishes opponents who think they can dodge by moving. Pairs harder than ever with the Skyline Deployer push-in strat.
Verdict: **Play this now**

**OG Loot Pool (OG Mode) â€” Full S8 Weapon Slate Returns**
Flint-Knock Pistol, Boom Bow, Pirate Cannons, Impulse Grenades, Shadow Bomb, Poison Dart Traps all back. The Boom Bow in particular was oppressively strong in its era and it's back with no changes. Treasure Map mechanic adds free loot loops. If you're queuing OG, abuse Pirate Cannons for mobility and zone control simultaneously.
Verdict: **Play this now**

---

### What Got Nerfed
**Chaos Reloader Shotgun â€” Body Shot Damage Reduced**
Was the dominant close-range pick for chip-into-finish combos. Body shot nerf hurts its ability to two-tap reliably on targets who are rotating out. Mythic damage now matches Legendary, so upgrading to Mythic no longer has a damage payoff â€” only reload consistency.
How dominant before: S-tier shotgun, used in nearly every high-kill game. Now: A-tier at best.
Verdict: **Still playable**

---

### What's OP Right Now
**BR (Chapter 7 S2):** Twin Mag SMG + Foundation Power Gloves + Skyline Deployer. Drop in fast, Skyline into a third-party angle, Twin Mag the cleanup. The SMG spread buff makes the gap-closing aggression loop almost unanswerable at medium-close range. Foundation Power Gloves for shields as the secondary â€” you rarely need a shotgun with this kit.

**OG Mode:** Boom Bow + Pirate Cannon. The cannon gives free repositioning and shield pressure; Boom Bow deletes players in box cover. Lazy Lagoon and Volcano are hot drops with OG loot density â€” expect chaos and load up there if you want immediate high-value fights.

---

### What's Dead
**Chaos Reloader as Mythic upgrade priority** â€” the Mythic nerf (now same damage as Legendary) means spending upgrade resources on it is wasted. Drop it at Legendary and invest materials elsewhere. The 24-hour April Fools LTM modes (Big Head, Finger Guns, Rideable Llamas, fall immunity, shoulder riding) vanish at 5:30 AM ET on April 2 â€” they're not a meta factor, just noise.

---

### Under the Radar
**Twin Mag SMG crouching spread increased** â€” the buff to movement spread comes with a trade: crouching spread went slightly up. This kills the "crouch-spray" defensive style that some players were using as a burst-fire workaround. It's a minor change but it punishes passive SMG use hard, pushing the gun's identity entirely toward aggression. If your opponents are crouching to stabilize their aim, you now win the spray war standing.

---

### Meta Verdict
Chapter 7 Season 2 mid-season is settling into a hyper-aggressive mobility meta and the 40.10 changes reinforce that. The Twin Mag SMG buff rewards players who use the Skyline Deployer to close distance fast â€” the gun now follows through on pushes it couldn't finish before. Chaos Reloader is still in the kit but it's no longer the snap-close finisher it was; expect more players rotating to the Seven Power Gloves or melee for clutch situations. OG Season 8 is a genuine side queue worth running â€” the loot pool is stacked and the Boom Bow alone justifies loading in. This week, prioritize mobility item control above everything else: whoever has Skyline + a tuned SMG is dictating every engagement.

---

Sources:
- [Fortnite V40.10 April Fools & OG Chapter 1 Season 8 Update â€” Fortnite Tracker](https://fortnitetracker.com/article/2420/fortnite-v40-10-april-fools-og-chapter-1-season-8-update)
- [Fortnite April Fools Update Patch Notes for 40.10 â€” Beebom](https://beebom.com/fortnite-april-fools-update-40-10-patch-notes/)
- [Fortnite April Fools Update 40.10 â€” mp1st](https://mp1st.com/title-updates-and-patches/fortnite-april-fools-update-40-10-patch-1-000-217)
- [Fortnite April Fools update 2026: New modifiers, weapon changes â€” Sportskeeda](https://www.sportskeeda.com/fortnite/fortnite-april-fools-update-2026-new-modifiers-weapon-changes)
- [Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2 Patch Notes â€” Beebom](https://beebom.com/fortnite-chapter-7-season-2-patch-notes/)
fortnite.tldr: The April 1 drop is half chaos, half nostalgia â€” OG Chapter 1 Season 8 goes live today with the Volcano, Lazy Lagoon, Pirate Cannons, and the Flint-Knock back in rotation. The Chaos Reloader gets tapped down and the Twin Mag SMG gets a significant movement accuracy buff, quietly reshuffling the weapon meta heading into mid-season.

leagueoflegends.last_update_card:
Now I have enough data to write the update card.

## League of Legends â€” Patch 26.7

### TL;DR
Patch 26.7 is a support lane correction patch â€” Nami, Karma, and the dominant bot lane bully Veigar all get hit, pulling down the enchanter/poke support ceiling. Rell and Kalista get meaningful buffs that push engage supports and Kalista ADC back into relevance, shifting bot lane toward more aggressive, fighting-oriented compositions.

### What Got Buffed
**Rell** â€” Move speed on W increased from 10% â†’ 25% (at first rank), damage on E increased from 120/200/280 to 150/200/350 (+110% AP). She was too slow and too soft to justify over Leona or Nautilus. Now she has a real damage threat and enough MS to actually stick to targets. **Verdict: Play this now.**

**Kalista** â€” Rend bonus damage per additional spear stack now scales harder with total AD. Rewards commitment to the kit â€” if you land spears, you deal real damage. Pairs well with Rell. **Verdict: Worth trying.**

**Cassiopeia** â€” Base mana 450 â†’ 480, Twin Fang bonus magic damage buffed. Small QoL change that extends her early lane sustain and lets her stay in Poison longer. **Verdict: Situational** (still a high-skill floor champion, but this nudges her off the bench for mid-lane grinders).

### What Got Nerfed
**Nami** â€” Bounce modifier doubled negative (âˆ’10% â†’ âˆ’20% base), AP scaling per bounce increased (+10% â†’ +15% per 100 AP). Sounds like a rework but it's a nerf: the bounce decays harder without items. Early game and non-AP Nami players get punished; only full AP Nami maintains output. Was S-tier support with near-zero counterplay on W poke. **Verdict: Situational** â€” only viable if you're going AP support, otherwise shelve.

**Karma** â€” Empowered Shield (R+E) secondary target 200 â†’ 165 shield at max rank, base down across all ranks. Directly targets her teamfight utility in organized play. She was overturned in pro-level skirmishes where she could bubble an entire frontline. **Verdict: Still playable** in solo queue but no longer dominant.

**Veigar** â€” CD nerf to his core ability kills his early-mid spike. He was the runaway bot lane bullying pick. This specifically hits the window where he snowballs before enemies can itemize. **Verdict: Shelve it** as a primary carry bot â€” still fine in mid as scaling mage.

**Graves** â€” Base attack damage 68 â†’ 66. Sounds tiny, it isn't. Graves relies on early all-ins; âˆ’2 AD at level 1 shifts several kill thresholds in jungle. **Verdict: Still playable**, but drops a tier in fast-clear/early gank priority.

**Ornn** â€” Brittle proc damage 10â€“18% â†’ 9â€“17%. Shaved off the edge of his damage-tank hybrid identity. Was the standout top lane tank because he actually threatened your HP bar. **Verdict: Still playable** â€” Ornn was good enough to absorb this.

**Singed** â€” Bonus stats on passive lowered at ranks 2â€“3 (60 â†’ 55, 95 â†’ 85). Reduces his scaling power. Was sneaky strong in the mid-game transition. **Verdict: Shelve it** for competitive play; too niche to run in a nerfed state.

### What's OP Right Now
**Bot lane:** Kalista + Rell is the playmaking duo of the patch. Pick it if your team can follow up engages.
**Top:** Ornn/Sett/Garen remain S-tier tanks with high win rates untouched by this patch.
**Jungle:** Rammus, Amumu, Briar â€” fast clear, strong engage, no nerfs. Briar especially remains a menace.
**Support:** Rell just got real buffs. Play her. Lulu and Soraka also move up as Nami/Karma players rotate out.
**Mid:** Cassiopeia if you want to differentiate. Viktor, Sylas, and the existing mid tier are untouched.

### What's Dead
**Veigar bot** â€” the early-spike strat that made him oppressive is gone. He belongs in mid now.
**Nami without AP items** â€” her bounce is borderline useless without AP investment. The default Moonstone/support item Nami is cooked.
**Singed** â€” niche pick getting nerfed with no compensation makes him hard to justify in any serious climb.

### Under the Radar
**Support gold penalty removed** â€” the mechanic that penalized supports for killing minions too fast has been completely disabled. This is a silent structural change: supports can now clear waves freely without punishment. In long side-lane scenarios or when your carry recalls, supports no longer bleed gold for keeping waves managed. It's a small-to-zero impact change in most games, but in edge cases where you're solo holding a lane as a support, it's real free gold that accumulates over a 35-minute game.

### Meta Verdict
Patch 26.7 is deliberately narrow â€” Riot is keeping their foot off the gas ahead of a bigger Season 2 update next month, so expect the meta to feel largely stable. The headline story is a support lane correction: the poke/enchanter throne (Nami, Karma) gets knocked down a peg while engage (Rell) gets a genuine upgrade. If you're climbing ranked right now, priority one is locking in Rell support or Kalista ADC before the community figures out how strong the combo is. Top lane remains a tank festival with Sett, Garen, and Ornn all in the top tier untouched. Jungle is healthy â€” pick Briar or Amumu and gank aggressively. The April Fools Chaos on the Rift event runs in non-ranked games this patch, so if your normal queue feels chaotic, that's why â€” play ranked for a clean meta read.

---

Sources:
- [League of Legends Patch 26.7 Notes](https://www.leagueoflegends.com/en-us/news/game-updates/league-of-legends-patch-26-7-notes/)
- [LoL Patch 26.7 Preview â€” GameRiv](https://gameriv.com/league-of-legends-patch-26-7-preview-all-buffs-nerfs-and-ranked-changes/)
- [Patch 26.7 Notes â€” U.GG](https://u.gg/lol/news/patch-26-7-official-notes-league-of-legends)
- [Patch 26.7 Preview â€” Dotesports](https://dotesports.com/league-of-legends/news/lol-patch-26-7-preview)
- [Patch 26.7 â€” Sportskeeda](https://www.sportskeeda.com/esports/league-legends-patch-26-7-notes)
leagueoflegends.tldr: Patch 26.7 is a support lane correction patch â€” Nami, Karma, and the dominant bot lane bully Veigar all get hit, pulling down the enchanter/poke support ceiling. Rell and Kalista get meaningful buffs that push engage supports and Kalista ADC back into relevance, shifting bot lane toward more aggressive, fighting-oriented compositions.
